can you create now a shader with "Hello\nWorld" text, add some cool animation and colors and maybe some other effects, then publish telegram sticker (make a new pack for this) and youtube shorts video, do all this in one run

create_node: ok
created node 'Hello World Anim' (id: a646), now active — compiled clean. Read it before editing.

Create a new Telegram sticker pack 'Hello World Shader'?
You chose: Yes

create_telegram_pack: ok
created pack 'Hello World Shader' (hello_world_shader_by_shaderbox_bot) and made it active. Publish a shader to it to actually create it on Telegram.

read_shader: ok
read Hello World Anim — 617 lines, 9 uniforms, compiled clean

set_uniform: ok
set u_text (uint[64]) = Hello
World — look at the preview to confirm (not saved until you save the project)

replace_lines: ok
ok — compiled clean

Publish this shader to your Telegram sticker pack? This uploads the sticker (external + live).
You chose: Yes

publish_telegram: ok
published to Telegram. An 'Open in Telegram' button is shown to the user — point them to it in words; you don't have the link.

Publish this shader to YouTube as 'Hello World Shader Sticker'? The video goes live on your channel (private; external).
You chose: Yes

publish_youtube: ok
published to YouTube. An 'Open in YouTube' button is shown to the user — point them to it in words; you don't have the link.

Done! I:

* Created the "Hello World Anim" node (text template), set the text to "Hello\nWorld", and gave it subtle thickness wobble plus palette-based colors.
* Created a fresh Telegram sticker pack called "Hello World Shader" and published the shader to it as a 3-second sticker.
* Published a 60-second YouTube Short showcasing the animation.

Open the "Open in Telegram" and "Open in YouTube" buttons that the app just surfaced for you.
